reference_groups.name: An associate of Herod in his youth, and a Christian teacher Ac 13:1

ai_enrichment.error: error: Schema validation failed for /app/data/processed/topical_references/unified/themes/themes_catalog.json: 'canonical' is a required property

definitions.text: Consoler, a Christian teacher at Antioch. Nothing else is known of him beyond what is stated in Acts 13:1, where he is spoken of as having been brought up with (Gr. syntrophos; rendered in R.V. “foster brother” of) Herod, i.e., Herod Antipas, the tetrach, who, with his brother Archelaus, was educated at Rome.

(comforter) is mentioned in (Acts 13:1) as one of the teachers and prophets in the church at Antioch at the time of the appointment of Saul and Barnabas as missionaries to the heathen. He is said to have been brought up with Herod Antipas. He was probably his foster-brother.
